data.title: KBRA Assigns Preliminary Ratings to Fannie Mae’s CAS 2026-R03
data.description: In New York, KBRA has assigned preliminary ratings to 58 classes from Connecticut Avenue Securities Trust 2026-R03 (CAS 2026-R03), a credit risk sharing transaction. The total note offering for CAS 2026-R03 amounts to $629,983,000. This pool mainly comprises loans with original loan-to-value (LTV) ratios ranging from over 80% to 97%. The Reference Pool contains 55,708 residential mortgage loans, showcasing an outstanding principal balance of around $20.0 billion as of the most recent update.

data.title: Sonic Fire Tech Included in Los Angeles County’s First-Ever Approved 3D-Printed Concrete Home Permit, Lands NFPA 13D-Equivalent Rating, and Wows San Bernardino County Fire in Live Test
data.description: A Los Angeles-based company, Sonic Fire Tech, has achieved significant success in developing infrasound-based fire defense systems inspired by NASA. Their product, the Sonic Home Defense system, has been designated as the optional fire protection element in Los Angeles County's first-ever approval for a 3D building permit. This milestone permit was granted for a 3D-printed concrete home constructed by PCI Build in Monrovia. This development signals a groundbreaking advancement in residential fire protection in California.

data.title: Intero Welcomes Nicole Aissa as Vice President and Managing Officer of Menlo Park and Los Altos Offices
data.description: Intero, a subsidiary of HomeServices of America, Inc., has named Nicole Aissa as the Vice President and Managing Officer of its Menlo Park and Los Altos branches. A Bay Area native, Aissa brings a wealth of real estate experience, having held leadership positions within top Bay Area brokerages for over ten years. She has a proven track record of success in areas such as recruitment, retention, and agent expansion. Aissa's appointment signifies Intero's commitment to further growth and excellence in the region.

data.title: Badger Meter Analysts Slash Their Forecasts After Q1 Earnings
data.description: Badger Meter Inc (NYSE: BMI) released its first-quarter results, showing a 9% decline in sales to $202.3 million, falling short of expectations. Diluted EPS dropped from $1.30 to 93 cents, also below projected figures. CEO Kenneth C. Bockhorst stated that the results aligned with their expectations, attributing the revenue decrease to project timing variability and customer order patterns rather than a shift in demand or their competitive position. In a separate announcement, Badger Meter disclosed a $100 million acquisition deal for UK-based UDlive, with the closing anticipated by the end of the specified period.

data.title: Home State Health and Centene Foundation Launch Medical Respite Program in St. Louis
data.description: Collaborating with Three Steps Home, a program has been established in St. Louis to aid residents facing homelessness. On April 20, 2026, Home State Health, a top health insurer offering affordable coverage to Missourians, joined hands with the Centene Foundation. This partnership aims to support individuals without permanent housing.

data.title: Cure Companies Welcomes Bombas Co-Founder Dave Heath as Strategic Investor and Board Member
data.description: Heath has joined Cure Companies following a $5 million raise, contributing capital, conviction, and taking on an active strategic role in the company's upcoming phase of regional expansion. The announcement was made on April 20, 2026, in Tenafly, NJ. Cure Companies is a hospitality and lifestyle holding firm known for Padel United Sports Club.

data.title: Havila Shipping ASA: Annual report 2025
data.description: Today, the financial statements for 2025 of both the Group and the parent company of Havila Shipping ASA were approved by the Board of Directors. The numbers align with the preliminary accounts published on 26 February 2026. For further information, please contact Chief Executive Officer Njål Sævik at +47 909 35 722 or Chief Financial Officer Arne Johan Dale at +47 909 87 706.

data.title: Claritas Rx Introduces Omnichannel CRM to Transform Patient Services and Speed Access to Specialty Therapies
data.description: In South San Francisco, a company called Claritas Rx has introduced a new Patient Services Omnichannel CRM. This innovative solution allows clients to interact efficiently and consistently throughout the entire patient care network. From specialty and independent pharmacies to hubs, field teams, case managers, and other crucial parties, the platform accelerates patient assistance and simplifies communication. This leads to enhanced patient experiences at all care stages. The new system aims to unify engagement processes, offering a swifter and improved care journey.

data.title: Blue Shield of California Announces New Leader to Drive Nonprofit Health Plan's Innovative Pharmacy Services for Members
data.description: Hayley Park, PharmD, an experienced industry professional, has been appointed as the Senior Vice President and Chief Pharmacy Officer for a health plan based in Oakland, California. Blue Shield of California disclosed on April 20, 2026, that Park will oversee the organization's prescription drug initiatives. This move highlights the company's commitment to enhancing pharmaceutical services.

data.title: Blo Blow Dry Bar Expands into Kentucky with Local Franchise Owner
data.description: A resident of Louisville is set to launch the initial branch of Blo Blow Dry Bar in the city's East End area. This move marks the first expansion of the North American blow dry bar franchise into Kentucky. The opening is scheduled for April 20, 2026, with further plans for establishing multiple units in the future.

data.title: FERRARI N.V.: PERIODIC REPORT ON THE BUYBACK PROGRAM
data.description: Italian town Maranello has revealed that Ferrari N.V. ("Ferrari" or "Company") has bought back Euro 250 million worth of common shares as part of a Euro 3.5 billion multi-year share repurchase program planned to be completed by 2030. This recent acquisition marks the second tranche of the buyback initiative, which was initially disclosed during the 2025 Capital Markets Day. The purchased shares will be reported daily on the Euronext Milan (EXM) stock exchange.

data.title: CPI will announce Q1 2026 results on May 5, 2026.
data.description: In the latest update, it was reported that 7 new cases of COVID-19 have been confirmed in the city, bringing the total number of cases to 42. Additionally, 3 more COVID-19 related deaths were recorded, raising the total death toll to 9. The city officials have emphasized the importance of following safety measures to prevent further spread of the virus. So far, 2500 COVID-19 tests have been conducted in the city, with 10% of them returning positive. The local health department is working diligently to trace and isolate those who may have come into contact with the infected individuals.
FRANCE'S MACRON BELIEVES BOTH THE US AND IRAN ARE WRONG ABOUT BLOCKADES IN THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ.

data.title: Informa TechTarget Expands its Financial Services Industry Coverage to the United Kingdom
data.description: Informa TechTarget expanded its financial services industry coverage to the United Kingdom, with this recent announcement made from Newton, Mass. The extension aims to enhance the company's reach and impact within the UK market, demonstrating its commitment to providing valuable insights and information to professionals in this sector. This strategic move reflects the growing importance of the UK in the financial services landscape, as well as the company's dedication to serving a wider audience.
U.S. STOCKS EXTEND FALL, NASDAQ DOWN 1.00%

data.title: Nomic and Broad Clinical Labs Partner to Integrate Omni 1000 into Discovery and Translational Workflows
data.description: Nomic Bio has partnered with Broad Clinical Labs (BCL) to incorporate the Nomic platform into its multi-omic workflows, revolutionizing the delivery of scalable, high-throughput proteomics. With almost 1 million samples analyzed for biopharma and academic clients via its core profiling service, this collaboration aims to expand by introducing the platform to specific institutions for advancing new applications and workflows.

data.title: Building Beyond the Jobsite: Gray's Commitment to the Construction Industry
data.description: In Lexington, Kentucky, on April 20, 2026, the construction and engineering company Gray is actively investing in the construction sector's future. The firm, known nationwide, is making significant commitments to enhance various aspects of the industry. This includes initiatives in higher education, workforce development, professional associations, and jobsite safety. This strategic investment underscores Gray's dedication to fostering growth and innovation in construction.

data.title: History repeating as water systems become casualties of modern war, warns Hydrachem
data.description: Recent incidents of conflicts globally are once more jeopardizing crucial water facilities, sparking concerns that millions of people could lose access to clean water and sanitation. Data from the Pacific Institute's Water Conflict Chronology reveals thousands of such instances, highlighting the urgent need to address this issue.

data.title: Autoliv Analysts Boost Their Forecasts After Strong Q1 Earnings
data.description: Autoliv, Inc. (NYSE: ALV) reported strong first-quarter results with adjusted earnings per share at $2.05, surpassing the estimated $1.89. The quarterly sales stood at $2.753 billion, reflecting a 6.8% increase compared to the previous year and exceeding the expected $2.605 billion. CEO Mikael Bratt noted an improvement in underlying profitability, evident by a 10% rise in gross profit despite a slight drop in adjusted operating income due to temporary factors. Anticipating consistent customer demand and no significant economic disruptions, the company forecasts approximately 0% organic sales growth for the full year 2026. Additional details can be found on Benzinga.com.
